codeReview.summary: Reviewed the mission and architecture guidance, the prior failed review, both handoffs, the fix transcript skeleton, the ts-worker skill, the original commit 1fe2a81589f3b139ea85f0aaf76555d4dee3b893, and the fix commit 1bb1853835e9684f4f0e2375eb839fc1f2854ef4. The fix replaces the leaking `lastSeenChats` fan-out with per-user/per-session notification routes in `notificationChats`, registers those routes only when a user attaches, and adds unit/integration coverage proving detached-session notifications stay isolated from other users and from unrelated `/list` chats. That adequately resolves the prior blocking notification-isolation leak, and I did not find a new blocking regression in the reviewed scope.

issues.description: Both auto-detach reply paths still send `Auto-detached: local input detected.` with a trailing period (also repeated at line 852), so the user-visible text remains slightly off-spec relative to `validation-contract.md:192` and `features.json:702`. This discrepancy was already noted in the prior review and remains unfixed here.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs still describe the bot as holding only the in-memory user→session attach mapping, but the reviewed fix now also depends on an in-memory per-user/per-session notification route map that persists after detach to scope background notifications. Future workers reasoning about notification behavior or restart semantics would have to rediscover that state from code.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:88-90,159 says the bot is stateless except for attachment mapping; the reviewed implementation stores detached notification routes in bot/src/bot-service.ts:90,387,824-835.

summary: Pass. Commit `1bb1853835e9684f4f0e2375eb839fc1f2854ef4` fixes the prior blocking multi-user notification leak by limiting background alerts to session-specific chat routes created by previous attaches, and the new tests cover both cross-user isolation and the unrelated-`/list` case. I found no new blocking problems in the reviewed scope, though the earlier non-blocking auto-detach punctuation mismatch is still present.
